codeReview.summary: Reviewed the fix feature metadata, prior failed review, completed-worker handoff, transcript skeleton, commit diff, and the current `src/backtest/walk_forward.py`, `src/model/calibration.py`, and `tests/test_walk_forward.py` code paths. The fix removes the hardcoded walk-forward `identity` default, sources both the API default and CLI choices from the shared calibration module, and adds regression coverage proving omitted overrides now follow the live `DEFAULT_CALIBRATION_METHOD` path. I did not find a remaining blocking defect in scope for the calibration-parity failure.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared state still gives mixed guidance about the canonical calibration method: the mission brief says the stack is isotonic-calibrated, while the architecture/library notes and live code now establish Platt as the active default. That ambiguity likely made it easier for the original backtest feature to drift to an `identity` default.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `C:\Users\bills\.factory\missions\83c0c194-72d1-4821-8b08-68a3497c3590\mission.md:7,33` still says 'Isotonic calibration'; `C:\Users\bills\Documents\Personal Projects\MLBPrediction2026\.factory\library\architecture.md:55-56` and `C:\Users\bills\Documents\Personal Projects\MLBPrediction2026\.factory\library\ml-calibration.md:3-5` record Platt as the current default; `src/model/calibration.py:43-44` defines `DEFAULT_CALIBRATION_METHOD = "platt"` and supported methods.

summary: Pass. Commit `496351f8b10d` adequately resolves the prior calibration-parity failure by aligning walk-forward defaults with `src.model.calibration` and covering both the Python API and CLI no-override paths. I did not find a new blocking issue in the reviewed scope, though the mission-level calibration narrative is still inconsistent across shared docs.
